proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e=1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e=0`, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.

Cell lines with `egfr_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification=0`, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.

Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=0`, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted BRAF V600E coefficient was -0.5545 on dependency_BRAF; BRAF-mutant lines were substantially more dependent.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted EGFR amplification coefficient was -0.3489 on dependency_EGFR; EGFR-amplified lines were more dependent.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ERBB2 amplification coefficient was -0.4325 on dependency_ERBB2; ERBB2-amplified lines were more dependent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation=0`, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss=1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss=0`, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted KRAS mutation coefficient was -0.3095 on dependency_KRAS; KRAS-mutant lines were strongly more KRAS-dependent.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted NF1 loss coefficient was -0.0954 on dependency_RIT1; NF1-loss lines were more RIT1-dependent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage='colorectal'` and `apc_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, and high `wnt_activity_score` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: dependency_KIF18A for lineage == colorectal and apc_mutation == 1 versus all other lines
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal APC-mutant subgroup (n=3925) had mean dependency_KIF18A -0.9425 versus -0.2527 in other lines, a -0.6899 difference.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: dependency_KIF18A for colorectal APC-mutant lines with wnt_activity_score >= Q75 (0.819) versus all other lines
analyses.result_summary: Adding high WNT activity sharpened the KIF18A signal: n=3133, mean -1.1119 versus -0.2530 in other lines, a -0.8589 difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within `lineage='colorectal'` and `apc_mutation=1` cell lines, `smad4_loss=1` suppresses KIF18A dependency, making `dependency_KIF18A` less negative than in otherwise similar `smad4_loss=0` lines.
Cell lines with `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `smad4_loss=0`, and high `wnt_activity_score` have the most negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores among tested KIF18A subgroups.

analyses.code: OLS interaction model: dependency_KIF18A ~ colorectal*apc_mutation*smad4_loss + colorectal*apc_mutation*high_wnt + covariates
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal:APC:SMAD4-loss interaction coefficient was +0.8484, showing that SMAD4 loss made KIF18A scores much less negative in APC-mutant colorectal lines.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_KIF18A ~ I(colorectal & apc_mutation & !smad4_loss & high_wnt) + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate
analyses.result_summary: The complete subgroup coefficient was -1.0386 after covariate adjustment; subgroup mean was -1.2990 versus -0.2530 in the complement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` or high `copy_number_8q24` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other lines, consistent with MYC/8q24-linked ribosomal stress vulnerability.
Cell lines with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than other lines, after accounting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RPL11 ~ myc_amplification + high_8q24 + high_growth + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate
analyses.result_summary: Both MYC features were independently associated with stronger RPL11 dependency: myc_amplification coefficient -0.0472 and high_8q24 coefficient -0.0667.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_DHX9 ~ high_myc_rna + myc_amplification + high_growth + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score+growth_rate
analyses.result_summary: High MYC RNA was independently associated with more negative DHX9 dependency (coefficient -0.0264), while MYC amplification itself was not independent once MYC RNA was included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores, reflecting stronger dependency on core transcriptional machinery in faster-growing cell lines.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and higher `cas9_activity_score` are associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores, without a strong named oncogenic subgroup.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_POLR2A ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + C(lineage)+C(culture_type)+C(screen_batch)+cas9_activity_score
analyses.result_summary: Growth rate had a large negative POLR2A slope of -0.3046 per doubling/day after adjustment for lineage, culture, batch, and Cas9 activity.

analyses.code: OLS models for dependency_TMED10: growth_rate_doublings_per_day adjusted for lineage/culture/batch/Cas9, and cas9_activity_score adjusted for lineage/culture/batch/growth
analyses.result_summary: TMED10 showed negative associations with growth rate (slope -0.1010, p=1.65e-38) and Cas9 activity adjusted for growth (slope -0.1035, p=1.87e-15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among `braf_v600e=1` cell lines, `nf1_loss=1` weakens BRAF dependency, making `dependency_BRAF` less negative than in `braf_v600e=1` and `nf1_loss=0` lines.
The `erbb2_amplification=1` effect on `dependency_ERBB2` is stronger in `lineage_subtype='her2_enriched'` cell lines than in other subtypes.
The `kras_mutation=1` effect on `dependency_KRAS` is broadly negative across lineages rather than being confined to pancreatic or colorectal lineages.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e*nf1_loss + covariates
analyses.result_summary: The BRAF V600E by NF1-loss interaction was positive (+0.0120) but not significant (p=0.494), so NF1 loss did not reliably suppress BRAF dependency in this dataset.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification*her2_subtype + covariates
analyses.result_summary: The ERBB2 amplification by HER2-enriched interaction was -0.0645 (p=0.045), supporting a modest but significant additional ERBB2 dependency in HER2-enriched amplified lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation*C(lineage) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: KRAS mutation remained strongly negative, and lineage interaction terms were not significant after correction-scale inspection; the colorectal interaction was +0.0198 with p=0.302 and pancreatic interaction was +0.0144 with p=0.460.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The `nf1_loss=1` effect on `dependency_RIT1` is stronger in `lineage='brain'` cell lines than in other lineages.
Cell lines with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss*brain_lineage + covariates
analyses.result_summary: The NF1-loss by brain-lineage interaction was -0.0577 (p=0.0060), indicating stronger RIT1 dependency in NF1-loss brain lines.

analyses.code: Welch t-test and adjusted subgroup model for high MYC RNA and high growth
analyses.result_summary: High-MYC-RNA/high-growth lines had mean dependency_DHX9 -0.3892 versus -0.3297 in other lines (difference -0.0595); the adjusted subgroup coefficient was -0.0338 (p=4.05e-7).

analyses.code: Welch t-test and adjusted subgroup model for myc_amplification and high growth
analyses.result_summary: MYC-amplified/high-growth lines had mean dependency_RPL11 -0.3936 versus -0.2632 in other lines; the adjusted subgroup coefficient was -0.1085 (p=8.22e-47).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search, the best-supported `dependency_BRAF` subgroup is still `braf_v600e=1`, with more negative `dependency_BRAF` than all other cell lines.

In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search, the best-supported `dependency_EGFR` subgroup is `egfr_amplification=1`, with more negative `dependency_EGFR` than all other cell lines.

In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search, `erbb2_amplification=1` and especially `lineage_subtype='her2_enriched'` plus `erbb2_amplification=1` mark more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores.

In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search, the best-supported `dependency_KRAS` subgroup is `kras_mutation=1`, with more negative `dependency_KRAS` than all other cell lines and no required lineage predicate.
In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search, `nf1_loss=1` and `lineage='brain'` marks a more negative `dependency_RIT1` subgroup than `nf1_loss=1` alone.

In a systematic single- and pair-predicate heterogeneity search followed by refinement, `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `smad4_loss=0`, and high `wnt_activity_score` defines the most negative `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search, no named molecular subgroup has an independent negative `dependency_TMED10` effect after accounting for growth rate and Cas9 activity; the apparent high-growth/high-Cas9 subgroup should have near-zero adjusted effect.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search, high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` plus high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` defines a more negative `dependency_DHX9` subgroup.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search, `dependency_POLR2A` heterogeneity is dominated by high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`; a lung-adenocarcinoma high-growth subgroup should not remain independently significant after continuous growth adjustment.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search, `myc_amplification=1` plus high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` defines a more negative `dependency_RPL11` subgroup.

analyses.code: Enumerated binary/categorical predicates and Q25/Q75 continuous predicates, plus all pairs with adequate support; ranked subgroups by negative mean difference and Welch p-value.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest BRAF subgroup was braf_v600e=1: n=5574, mean -0.8047 versus -0.2495 in others, difference -0.5552.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_EGFR.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest EGFR subgroup was egfr_amplification=1: n=3880, mean -0.6010 versus -0.2524 in others, difference -0.3486.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_ERBB2 plus ERBB2*HER2 subtype interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: ERBB2 amplification had difference -0.4321 overall; the ERBB2-amplified HER2-enriched subgroup had mean -0.7525 versus -0.2944 in others, difference -0.4581.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_KRAS plus KRAS*lineage interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest interpretable KRAS driver was kras_mutation=1: n=11842, mean -0.5594 versus -0.2520, difference -0.3075; lineage interactions were small and not significant.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_RIT1 plus NF1*brain interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: NF1-loss brain lines had mean dependency_RIT1 -0.4047 versus -0.2650 in others, difference -0.1397, and the adjusted NF1*brain interaction was significant.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_KIF18A, then refined OLS interaction model over colorectal, APC, WNT, and SMAD4 loss.
analyses.result_summary: The complete colorectal/APC/SMAD4-intact/high-WNT subgroup had mean dependency_KIF18A -1.2990 versus -0.2530 in others, difference -1.0460.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_TMED10 followed by adjusted model for the best apparent high-growth/high-Cas9 subgroup with continuous growth and Cas9 covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The high-growth/high-Cas9 subgroup looked more negative unadjusted (difference -0.0417), but its adjusted subgroup coefficient was +0.0033 with p=0.651, so it was not an independent dependency subgroup.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_DHX9 followed by adjusted high-MYC-RNA/high-growth subgroup model.
analyses.result_summary: High-MYC-RNA/high-growth lines retained a negative adjusted DHX9 effect of -0.0338 (p=4.05e-7).

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_POLR2A followed by adjusted lung-adenocarcinoma/high-growth subgroup model with continuous growth covariate.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lung adenocarcinoma lines were more negative unadjusted (difference -0.1026), but after continuous growth adjustment the subgroup coefficient was -0.0166 with p=0.116; the independent signal is growth rate itself.

analyses.code: Same systematic subgroup scan for dependency_RPL11 followed by adjusted MYC-amplified/high-growth subgroup model.
analyses.result_summary: MYC-amplified/high-growth lines retained a strong negative adjusted RPL11 subgroup effect of -0.1085 (p=8.22e-47).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final set of adjusted models will retain negative dependency effects for the best-supported drivers of `dependency_BRAF`, `dependency_EGFR`, `dependency_ERBB2`, `dependency_KRAS`, `dependency_RIT1`, `dependency_KIF18A`, `dependency_DHX9`, `dependency_POLR2A`, and `dependency_RPL11`, while `dependency_TMED10` will show only growth/Cas9-associated variation and no independent molecular subgroup.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS models for each dependency outcome using the best-supported driver/subgroup and common covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted effects: BRAF/braf_v600e -0.5545; EGFR/egfr_amplification -0.3489; ERBB2/erbb2_amplification -0.4325; KRAS/kras_mutation -0.3095; RIT1/nf1_loss -0.0954; KIF18A/colorectal+APC+SMAD4-intact+high-WNT -1.0386; DHX9/high-MYC-RNA+high-growth -0.0338; POLR2A/growth-rate slope -0.3046; RPL11/MYC-amplified+high-growth -0.1085. TMED10 had growth/Cas9 slopes but no independent molecular subgroup.
